title: betula fruticosa subsp. ruprechtiana
intro: Betula fruticosa subsp. ruprechtiana, also known as the birch leaf white birch, is a subspecies of the Betula fruticosa tree, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Betula fruticosa subsp. ruprechtiana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cold and flu symptoms, and treat skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for medicinal purposes.
